description: Please subscribe to - www.youtube.com/0nly1truth Type "Alzuridin" in the Archive search bar above to watch/listen/download more free Islamic files. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- In the name of Allaah, the Beneficient, the Merciful. The Noble Qur'aan is the last & final Divine revelation & universal Message to mankind confirming that which came before it, revealed by Allaah (the ONE Creator besides Whom none has the right to be worshipped). Islaam is the onle accepted way which has a combination of various meanings: * Peace * Submission * Surrender * Obedience * Purity Islaam is to commit one's self totally to God alone, making one's will subservient to & in complete harmony with the will of God. In simple words, a Muslim is one who submits to the will of God & does not disobey Him in word or action. The word "Allaah" is the perfect description of the "One God" of monotheism for Jews, Christians & Muslims! Is "Allaah" only for Islam and Muslims? [No! It is for All Three Abrahamic Faiths.] "Allaah" is the same word used by Christian Arabs & Jewish Arabs in their Bible, centuries before Islaam came. On page one [1] of Genesis in the Old Testament, we find the word "Allah" seventeen [17] times. In the introduction of the Arabic Bible, you will find samples of the different languages they have translated. For Arabic they have translated the famous verse in the New Testament from the Gospel John 3;16 - "For God so loved the world..." - and the word the translators used in Arabic for "God" is the very same word used by Muslims around the planet, "Allaah." Where Does the word "Allaah" Come From? "Allaah" comes from the Arabic word "elah" a god or something worshiped. This word (elah) can be made plural (gods), as in "aleha" and it can be male or female just as the word in English can be "goddess." "Allaah" comes from "elaha" but it brings more clarification & understanding. Allaah = Has no gender (not male & not female), "He" is used only out of respect & dignity - not for gender Allaah = Always singular - Never plural, "We" is used only as the "Royal WE" just as in English for royalty Allaah = Means "The Only One to be Worshipped" Continuity of Message: Allaah (the Exalted) Has sent his teaching to our world through his messengers at different periods in our history. He revealed to: * Dawood (David) - the Zaboor (the Psalms) * Moosa (Moses) - Tawraat (the Torah) * 'Eesa (Jesus) - Injeel (the Gospel) Six centuries after 'Eesa ibn Maryam ('alayhimus-Salaam) Allaah revealed His final Message; the Noble Qur'aan through His last Messenger, Muhammad (Sallallaahu 'alayhi wa Sallam), to be a guide to all people until the end of the world (i.e. The Day of Judgement). {Say (O Muhammad): "We believe in Allaah (One God) & in what has been sent down to us & what was sent down to Ibraheem (Abraham), Ismaa'eel (Ishmael), Is'haaq (Isaac), Ya'qoob (Jacob) & al-Asbaat [the twelve sons of Ya'qoob] & what was given to Moosa (Moses), 'Eesa (Jesus) & the Prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between one another among them & to Him (Allaah) we have submitted (in Islaam)."} (al-Qur'aan; 3.84) Pillars of Islam: A man who has consciously & verbally accepted these beliefs is expected to follow them by actions which will complete his ISLAAM. There are five pillars of Islaam. * The Shahaadah (Declaration of Faith) - to Bear Witness to the following: لا إله إلا الله محمد رسول الله "Ash-hadu an lā ʾilāha ʾillà l-Lāh, wa ash-hadu anna Muḥammadur-rasūlullāh" (I testify that None has the right to be worshipped but Allaah, and I testify that Muhammad is the messenger of Allaah.) * Salaah - to pray to Allaah (alone) five times a day at stated times. * Zakat - to pay 2.5% of his or her annual surplus wealth as a religious duty & a purifying sum to be spent on poorer sections of the community. * Sawm - to fast between dawn & sunset during the month of Ramadan. * Hajj - to undertake the Pilgrimage to Makkah once in a life time, if one can afford it financially & is able physically. Besides these pillars every action which is done with the awareness that it fulfills the will of Allaah (the Exalted) is also considered an action of worship Inshaa Allaah! (God Willing) The most important aspect of Islaam is Tawheed (the Oneness of God): {Say (O Muhammad): "He is Allaah, the One & Only; Allaah, the Eternal, Absolute; He begetteth not, nor is He begotten; And there is none like unto Him."} (al-Qur'aan; 112) And the Gravest sin of ALL sins is Shirk (i.e. to associate partners with Allaah, e.g. to falsely claim He has a son, is part of a Trinity or that someone or something else in existence has divine attributes alongside Allaah). Anyone that dies upon Tawheed will enter Jannah (the Gardens of Paradise) Inshaa Allaah! But anyone who dies upon Shirk will enter Jahannam (Hell-Fire). {Verily, Allaah forgiveth not (the sin of) joining other gods with Him; but He forgiveth whom He pleaseth other sins than this: one who associates partners with Allaah, Hath strayed far, far away (from the right).} (al-Qur'aan; 4.116) There can only be One Truth! {Say (O Muhammad ﷺ): "Truth has (now) arrived, and Falsehood perished: for Falsehood is (by its nature) bound to perish."} (al-Qur'aan; 17.81) {Say (O Muhammad ﷺ): "I am only a man like you. It has been revealed to me that your ilah (God) is One ilah (God - i.e. Allaah). So whoever hopes for the Meeting with his Lord, let him work righteousness and associate none as a partner in the worship of his Lord."} (al-Qur'aan; 18.110) {Say (O Muhammad ﷺ): "If any men go astray, (Allaah) Most Gracious extends (the rope) to them, until, when they see the warning of Allaah (being fulfilled) - either in punishment or in (the approach of) the Hour,- they will at length realise who is worst in position, and (who is) weakest in forces!} (al-Qur'aan; 19.75) The last & final Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) said; "If anyone testifies that None has the right to be worshipped but Allaah alone (One God) Who has no partners, and that Muhammad is His Slave and His Apostle, and that Jesus is Allaah's Slave and His Apostle and His Word which He bestowed on Mary and a Spirit created by Him, and that Jannah (Paradise) is true, and that Jahannam (Hell) is true, Allaah will admit him into Paradise with the deeds which he had done even if those deeds were few." (Saheeh al-Bukhari) {Say, (O Muhammad ﷺ): "This is my way, I call to Allaah (i.e. to the testification that none has the right to be worshipped except God, alone, with no partner) upon certain knowledge - I, and those who follow me. I declare Allaah free and far removed from all that they associate as partners with Him, and I am free of those who worship anything else along with Him."} (al-Qur'aan; 12.108) ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ************************************ ~ THERE CAN ONLY BE ONE TRUTH! ~ ************************************ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Conveying the Truth to the entire world with the Niyah of a swift Da'wah & global Islamic awakening bi-idhnillaahi ta 'Ala. The Message of Allaah (Subhaanahu wa ta 'Ala) has no copyright and Allaah ('Azza wa Jal) is indeed Ar-Razzaaq. Therefore, make multitudinous copies and distribute freely. JazakumAllaahu-Khairan wa BarakAllaahu-Feekum Inshaa-Allaah! : ) ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Please type: "ALZURIDIN" in the Archive search bar above for many more beneficial Islamic files.
